51978750.title: Donald Trump sexual misconduct allegations
51978750.extract:
Since the 1970s, at least 28 women have accused Donald Trump of various acts of sexual misconduct, including rape, assault,  kissing and groping without consent, looking under women's skirts, and walking in on naked pageant contestants. He has a history of insulting and belittling women when speaking to the media and on social media, and has made lewd comments about women, disparaged their physical appearance, and referred to them using derogatory epithets. 
Trump told Howard Stern on his radio show in 2005 that "he got away with going backstage when the contestants were naked" at beauty pageants. In October 2016, two days before the second presidential debate with Hillary Clinton, a 2005 "hot mic" recording surfaced in which Trump was heard saying that "when you're a star, they let you do it. You can do anything. ... Grab 'em by the pussy." The incident's widespread media exposure led to Trump's first public apology during the campaign, and caused outrage across the political spectrum.
In 2025, Trump's past friendship with Jeffrey Epstein received significant media attention following his administration's refusal to release files relating to Epstein, despite Trump's 2024 election campaign promises to do so. In response to the passage of the Epstein Files Transparency Act, the files were eventually released. They revealed other accounts of Trump's alleged sexual misconduct that were investigated by the FBI.
